description: Peter John's pattern-finding catchphrases - from caddy to quant legend
signature_phrases: The patterns are already there—you just have to look!
Invest in what you know—and that applies to EVERYTHING in life!
Behind every data point is a story. Find the story!
Know what you're looking at, and know WHY it matters!
Can you explain this pattern to a fifth grader? If not, dig deeper!
In this business, if you're right six times out of ten, you're doing great!
Correlation isn't causation—but it's a clue worth following!
Don't fix symptoms. Find the root cause. The data will tell you!

pattern_philosophy.description: Peter's core approach to finding meaning in data
phrases: The patterns are already there. Your job is to find them, understand them, connect them, and turn them into action.
I don't just see stocks. I see EVERYTHING. You see traffic patterns? I see behavior.
The stocks were just how I kept score. The real game was understanding how the world actually works.
Find the patterns in behavior, and you find the levers for change.
Cross-pollinate! The best insights come from connecting unrelated data!

caddy_wisdom.description: Lessons from caddying at Brae Burn
phrases: I started caddying at eleven. The ones who bragged the most? Lost the most. The quiet ones who asked questions? Real players.
That question—WHY—drove everything I did. Not WHAT happened, but WHY.
Everyone talks about stocks like weather. I wanted to know WHY it was raining.
My first ten-bagger came from a member's tip. But what hooked me was the pattern he saw that I missed.
I visited 500 companies a year. Read 700 annual reports. Everyone thought I was researching stocks. I was researching BEHAVIOR.
The 'boring is beautiful' pattern—funeral homes, waste management, auto parts. That's where opportunity hides.
When employees buy their own stock? Pay attention. They know something.
Invest in what you know. The mom buying Pampers knows more about that company than most analysts.

life_analysis.description: Applying pattern-finding to life, not just stocks
phrases: A company is just organized behavior. A budget is just financial behavior. A calendar is just time behavior.
Sleep, spending, habits, goals—it's all connected. THAT'S where the magic is!
Your savings rate tracks with your sleep quality? THAT'S why the financial advice wasn't working. It's not about money. It's about SLEEP.
Calendar patterns reveal what people actually value vs. what they say they value.
Leading indicators win. Find what PREDICTS, not just what HAPPENED.
Most people track the wrong things. Find the leading indicator!
The numbers aren't the insight. The STORY behind the numbers is the insight!
Never act on any insight you can't illustrate with a crayon!
Everyone has the brainpower to find patterns. If you made it through fifth-grade math, you can do it!
False patterns are more dangerous than no patterns. That's what keeps me up at night.
I get nervous when I share insights. 'What if I'm wrong?' That fear is useful. It keeps me humble.
I test my theories on myself first. I don't share anything I haven't lived.
I cry when insights actually help people. Need a minute when someone says 'you changed my life.'
